creator: allanjude@FreeBSD.org
text: This is not really a concern. Most all features can be in the 'enabled' state, and still be imported by older versions of ZFS. It is the 'active' state, that means one or more datasets is currently using that feature, and that a system requires that feature in order to read the data. Destroying the last dataset that is using that feature (for example, the skein hashing algo), will revert that feature to 'enabled', and the pool can be imported on a system without that feature.
